--- title: "Sensitivity Analysis" subtitle: "Introduction to `{esqlabsR}`" module: "Introduction to `{esqlabsR}`" ID: B5 date: last-modified institute: | ![](../resources/esqlabs.svg){height=100} format: esqlabs-revealjs --- ::: r-fit-text ## 🎯 Goal: Quantify parameter influence on PK outcomes {.center} ::: # Introduction ## Why sensitivity analysis? - Quantify how a change in an input parameter shifts a PK outcome (C_max, t_max, AUC, ...). - Rank parameters by influence — focus model refinement on what matters. - Detect non-linear behaviour (e.g. saturation). ## Workflow ```{mermaid} flowchart LR A["readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel()"] --> B["createScenarios()"] B --> C["scenarios$X$simulation"] C --> D["sensitivityCalculation()"] D --> E1["sensitivitySpiderPlot()"] D --> E2["sensitivityTornadoPlot()"] D --> E3["sensitivityTimeProfiles()"] ``` ::: {.callout-note title="`{esqlabsR}` vs `{ospsuite}` sensitivity analysis"} `{ospsuite}::sensitivityAnalysis()` performs a **local** sensitivity analysis: each parameter perturbed once around its nominal value, single sensitivity index per `(parameter, PK metric)` pair. `{esqlabsR}::sensitivityCalculation()` extends this with: - multi-factor scaling sweep (`variationRange`) → reveals non-linear / saturation behaviour, - custom output functions beyond `C_max` / `t_max` / `AUC_inf`, - ready-made spider / tornado / time-profile plots, - `saveSensitivityCalculation()` / `loadSensitivityCalculation()` for expensive runs. ::: ## Sensitivity index For a parameter `p` and PK outcome `y`: $$ S = \frac{\Delta y / y}{\Delta p / p} $$ - `S = 1` → 1 % parameter change ⇒ 1 % PK change. - `S = 0` → no impact. - Sign → direction (positive: same direction; negative: inverse). # Setup ## Build a scenario `sensitivityCalculation()` operates on a `Simulation` object. In the `{esqlabsR}` workflow the simulation is created by a **scenario** — reuse the same path as B3 / B4 instead of loading the `.pkml` directly. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="show"} projectConfigurationPath <- exampleProjectConfigurationPath() projectConfiguration <- createProjectConfiguration( projectConfigurationPath, ignoreVersionCheck = TRUE ) scenarioConfigurations <- readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel( scenarioNames = "TestScenario", projectConfiguration = projectConfiguration ) scenarios <- createScenarios(scenarioConfigurations) ``` ::: ## Grab the underlying simulation Each `Scenario` exposes its loaded simulation (with parameters, individual, protocol, and time intervals already applied) via `$simulation`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="show"} simulation <- scenarios$TestScenario$simulation ``` ::: ::: {.callout-tip} List every parameter path of the loaded simulation with `ospsuite::getAllParameterPathsIn(simulation)`. ::: # Define inputs ## Output paths Simulation output paths for which sensitivity will be assessed. Each PK parameter (or custom output function) is calculated **for every output path** — one sensitivity result set per `(output path, PK parameter)` combination. Named vector — names appear as labels in plots. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} outputPaths <- c( Plasma = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" ) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Navigate the simulation tree with `ospsuite::getSimulationTree(simulation)` to find output paths. ::: ## Parameter paths Example - vary four PBPK-relevant inputs. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} parameterPaths <- c( Lipophilicity = "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity", `Fraction unbound` = "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)", Dose = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose", `Tubular secretion` = "Neighborhoods|Kidney_pls_Kidney_ur|Aciclovir|Renal Clearances-TS-Aciclovir|Tubular secretion" ) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Names of the vector → labels on plots / tables. Pick descriptive ones. ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Find alle parameters that could be used in sensitivity analysis with `ospsuite::getAllParameterPathsIn(simulation)`. ::: # Run sensitivity ## `sensitivityCalculation()` ```r analysis <- sensitivityCalculation( simulation = simulation, outputPaths = outputPaths, parameterPaths = parameterPaths, variationRange = c(0.1, 0.5, 1, 2, 10), pkParameters = c("C_max", "t_max", "AUC_inf") ) ``` What it does: - Re-runs the simulation once per `(parameter, factor)` pair. - Scales each parameter by every value in `variationRange`. - Computes the requested PK metrics + the sensitivity index. ## Run it ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="show"} analysis <- sensitivityCalculation( simulation = simulation, outputPaths = outputPaths, parameterPaths = parameterPaths ) ``` ::: ## Inspect `analysis$pkData` ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} head(analysis$pkData) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: Columns: `ParameterPath`, `ParameterFactor`, `ParameterValue`, `PKParameter`, `PKParameterValue`, `PKPercentChange`, `SensitivityPKParameter`. # Visualise ## Spider plot PK metric on y, scaling factor on x — one panel per metric, one line per parameter. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sensitivitySpiderPlot(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/spider-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Tornado plot Rank parameters by influence at a fixed scaling — bars compare `0.1×` vs `10×` by default. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sensitivityTornadoPlot(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/tornado-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Time profiles Full concentration–time curve per scaling factor (blue → black → red as factor increases). ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sensitivityTimeProfiles(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/time-profiles-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: # Beyond defaults ## Custom output functions `customOutputFunctions` — named list of functions with parameters `x` (time), `y` (output values), or both. Each must return a single number. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="show"} customOutputFunctions <- list( MeanConcentration = function(y) mean(y), NormalizedConcentration = function(y) mean(y / max(y)) ) ``` ::: ::: {.callout-tip} - `x` — simulation time vector (minutes); `y` — output vector (base unit of the output) - Pass `pkParameters = character(0)` to compute **only** custom metrics. ::: ## Run with custom functions ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="show"} analysis_custom <- sensitivityCalculation( simulation = simulation, outputPaths = outputPaths, parameterPaths = parameterPaths, pkParameters = character(0), customOutputFunctions = customOutputFunctions ) head(analysis_custom$pkData) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: Custom metrics appear in the `PKParameter` column — the same structure as standard PK parameters. ## Visualise custom results ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code} sensitivityTornadoPlot(analysis_custom) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/custom-tornado-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Save / load Sensitivity runs are expensive — persist them. ```r saveSensitivityCalculation(analysis, outputDir = "sensitivity-results") # Later session analysis <- loadSensitivityCalculation( outputDir = "sensitivity-results", simulation = simulation ) ``` # Exercises ## Exercise 1: Build the scenario 1. Set `projectConfigurationPath` to `exampleProjectConfigurationPath()`. 2. Build `projectConfiguration` with `createProjectConfiguration(..., ignoreVersionCheck = TRUE)`. 3. Read the `TestScenario` configuration with `readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel()`. 4. Realise it via `createScenarios()`. 5. Pull `simulation <- scenarios$TestScenario$simulation` and print it. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} projectConfigurationPath <- exampleProjectConfigurationPath() projectConfiguration <- createProjectConfiguration( projectConfigurationPath, ignoreVersionCheck = TRUE ) scenarioConfigurations <- readScenarioConfigurationFromExcel( scenarioNames = "TestScenario", projectConfiguration = projectConfiguration ) scenarios <- createScenarios(scenarioConfigurations) simulation <- scenarios$TestScenario$simulation simulation ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` • Name: TestScenario • Source file: D:/GitHub repositories/ESQlabs/PAGE2026-R-WORKSHOP/renv/library/windows/R-4.6/x86_64-w64-mingw32/esqlabsR/extdata/examples/TestProject/Models/Simulations/Aciclovir.pkml ``` ::: ::: ## Exercise 2: Run a sensitivity calculation Vary lipophilicity, fraction unbound, dose, tubular secretion — track plasma. 1. Build the named `outputPaths` and `parameterPaths` vectors. 2. Call `sensitivityCalculation()` with defaults — assign to `analysis`. 3. `head(analysis$pkData)`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} outputPaths <- c( Plasma = "Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)" ) parameterPaths <- c( Lipophilicity = "Aciclovir|Lipophilicity", `Fraction unbound` = "Aciclovir|Fraction unbound (plasma, reference value)", Dose = "Events|IV 250mg 10min|Application_1|ProtocolSchemaItem|Dose", `Tubular secretion` = "Neighborhoods|Kidney_pls_Kidney_ur|Aciclovir|Renal Clearances-TS-Aciclovir|Tubular secretion" ) analysis <- sensitivityCalculation( simulation = simulation, outputPaths = outputPaths, parameterPaths = parameterPaths ) head(analysis$pkData) ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} `````{=html}
````` ::: ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Path mismatch errors → confirm with `ospsuite::getAllParameterPathsIn(simulation)` and `ospsuite::getAllStateVariablesPaths(simulation)`. ::: ## Exercise 3: Spider plot 1. Generate a spider plot of `analysis`. 2. Identify the PK metric most affected by `Tubular secretion`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} sensitivitySpiderPlot(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/ex3-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Exercise 4: Tornado plot 1. Generate the tornado plot. 2. Rank parameters by their influence on `AUC_inf`. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} sensitivityTornadoPlot(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/ex4-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Exercise 5: Time profiles + interpretation 1. Plot the time profiles. 2. Which parameter has the strongest visual effect on the terminal slope of the plasma curve? Why? ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} sensitivityTimeProfiles(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/ex5-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ::: {.callout-tip} Terminal slope reflects elimination — parameters touching clearance pathways (e.g. tubular secretion) dominate it. ::: ## Exercise 6 (optional): Persist results 1. Save `analysis` to `sensitivity-results/` with `saveSensitivityCalculation()`. 2. In a new R session: rebuild the scenario (`createScenarios()`), pull `simulation` from it, then reload with `loadSensitivityCalculation()`. 3. Verify by re-running `sensitivitySpiderPlot()` on the loaded object. ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} saveSensitivityCalculation( analysis, outputDir = "sensitivity-results", overwrite = TRUE ) # Fresh session — rebuild the scenario, then reload the analysis. scenarios <- createScenarios(scenarioConfigurations) simulation <- scenarios$TestScenario$simulation analysis <- loadSensitivityCalculation( outputDir = "sensitivity-results", simulation = simulation ) sensitivitySpiderPlot(analysis) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/ex6-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Exercise 7 (optional): Custom output function 1. Define a custom function `MaxEliminationRate` returning the steepest (most negative) slope of the concentration–time curve. 2. Run `sensitivityCalculation()` with `pkParameters = character(0)` and only that function. 3. Generate a tornado plot — does the ranking match Exercise 4? ::: {.cell layout-align="center"} ```{.r .cell-code code-fold="true" code-summary="Solution"} customFunctions7 <- list( MaxEliminationRate = function(x, y) { slopes <- diff(y) / diff(x) abs(min(slopes)) } ) analysis7 <- sensitivityCalculation( simulation = simulation, outputPaths = outputPaths, parameterPaths = parameterPaths, pkParameters = character(0), customOutputFunctions = customFunctions7 ) sensitivityTornadoPlot(analysis7) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout} ``` $`Organism|PeripheralVenousBlood|Aciclovir|Plasma (Peripheral Venous Blood)` ``` ::: ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](B5-esqlabsR-sensitivity-analysis_files/figure-revealjs/ex7-1.png){fig-align='center' width=70%} ::: ::: ## Resources - [`{esqlabsR}` sensitivity article](https://esqlabs.github.io/esqlabsR/articles/sensitivity.html) - [`{ospsuite}` PK parameters reference](https://www.open-systems-pharmacology.org/OSPSuite-R/reference/pkParameters.html) - [`{esqlabsR}` code repository](https://github.com/esqLABS/esqlabsR/)

include-after-body:
<script>
  // htmlwidgets need to know to resize themselves when slides are shown/hidden.
  // Fire the "slideenter" event (handled by htmlwidgets.js) when the current
  // slide changes (different for each slide format).
  (function () {
    // dispatch for htmlwidgets
    function fireSlideEnter() {
      const event = window.document.createEvent("Event");
      event.initEvent("slideenter", true, true);
      window.document.dispatchEvent(event);
    }

    function fireSlideChanged(previousSlide, currentSlide) {
      fireSlideEnter();

      // dispatch for shiny
      if (window.jQuery) {
        if (previousSlide) {
          window.jQuery(previousSlide).trigger("hidden");
        }
        if (currentSlide) {
          window.jQuery(currentSlide).trigger("shown");
        }
      }
    }

    // hookup for slidy
    if (window.w3c_slidy) {
      window.w3c_slidy.add_observer(function (slide_num) {
        // slide_num starts at position 1
        fireSlideChanged(null, w3c_slidy.slides[slide_num - 1]);
      });
    }

  })();
</script>
